skill.name: claude-code-templates
skill.description: Provides reusable templates for Claude Code agents, skills, slash commands, and hook scripts.
skill.summary: Create Claude Code extension files from structured starter templates.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, placeholder commands, hidden Claude setup paths, and documentation URLs. The remaining real risk is an executable Bash hook template that parses hook input and writes local logs, so publication is acceptable with a script-use warning.

medium_findings.title: Executable Hook Script Template Requires Review
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The hook asset is a Bash script that reads stdin, runs jq, creates a Claude project log directory, and writes hook activity. This is legitimate template behavior, but copied hooks execute in user projects and can affect local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is an executable Bash template and the referenced lines perform input parsing and local log writes. The behavior is visible and intentional, with no evidence of exfiltration or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Directory Writes Are Documented Setup Steps
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious hidden-file access. The hidden paths are Claude Code installation and project configuration locations used by setup examples. They can overwrite user files if copied carelessly, so users should review destinations before running commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear inside copy and setup instructions for Claude Code templates. There is no evidence that the skill automatically writes to these locations.

low_findings.title: Recursive Delete Pattern Is a Commented Defensive Example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The detected rm -rf pattern appears only inside a commented example that blocks dangerous Bash commands. It is not executed by the template as shipped.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous command text is inside commented lines and the surrounding example describes blocking the operation. No active recursive delete command was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger Static Shell Detections
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for direct command execution. Most command detections are documentation examples, placeholders, tables, or code fences. They do not run unless a user deliberately copies and executes them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation or placeholder command text. No active runner or hidden execution path was found in these files.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Non-Exfiltrating References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for network risk. The hardcoded URLs point to public Claude Code, Anthropic, and Agent Skills documentation. No code sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed as references in Markdown comments or documentation sections. I found no request, curl, fetch, webhook, or credential transmission behavior.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for prompt injection. The agent template contains normal role-template language for a user-created expert agent. No text instructs the evaluator to ignore security analysis, override policy, or change risk levels.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The language is a visible placeholder for an agent persona template. I found no suspicious override wording or claims of special authority in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Hook Processes Tool Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The hook template reads JSON input from stdin and extracts tool fields with jq. This is expected for Claude hooks, but users should review changes before enabling copied scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The active shell code parses runtime hook input. It is a valid hook pattern, but it creates execution risk when customized.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hook Template Writes Local Log File
dangerous_patterns.description: The hook template creates a project Claude directory and appends hook activity to a log file. This is local logging, not data exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The active lines create a directory path and append a timestamped log entry. The destination is local and project-scoped by default.

content.value_statement: Building Claude Code extensions from scratch can lead to inconsistent file structure and missing metadata. This skill provides starter templates for agents, skills, commands, and hooks.
actual_capabilities: Provides a template for Claude Code expert agents.
Provides a template for Claude Code skills with frontmatter and references.
Provides a slash command template with usage, options, and examples.
Provides a Bash hook script template with JSON validation and exit code notes.
Documents setup locations for agents, skills, commands, and hooks.
Links to official Claude Code and Agent Skills documentation.
limitations: Templates still require manual editing before use.
The hook script requires jq when executed.
It does not validate completed extensions automatically.
It does not generate project-specific logic without user input.

use_cases.title: Standardize Extension Files
use_cases.description: Create consistent starter files for agents, skills, commands, and hooks across a team.

use_cases.title: Start a New Skill Quickly
use_cases.description: Use the skill template to define metadata, usage examples, references, and troubleshooting notes.

use_cases.title: Prototype Claude Hooks
use_cases.description: Begin with a hook script that parses JSON input, validates dependencies, and documents exit codes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent template to draft an expert agent for React component architecture. Keep placeholders visible where I need to decide details.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Skill Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill template to outline a Claude Code skill for Git workflow guidance. Include triggers, quick reference items, and limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Slash Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the command template to design a review command with options, execution flow, examples, and expected output.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Safe Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the hook script template to plan a PreToolUse hook that logs tool names and blocks path traversal. Explain every customization.

output_examples.input: I need a starting point for a new Claude Code skill.
output_examples.output: A structured skill draft with metadata, quick reference tasks, usage examples, troubleshooting rows, and links for further documentation.

output_examples.input: I want a reusable slash command format for team reviews.
output_examples.output: A command outline with usage syntax, execution steps, available options, examples, and a clear description of expected results.

output_examples.input: I need a hook template that I can customize safely.
output_examples.output: A hook plan that validates JSON input, checks dependencies, logs locally, and documents blocking and non-blocking exit codes.

best_practices: Review every generated hook script before making it executable.
Replace all placeholders with specific names, triggers, and examples before publishing.
Keep templates small and move long reference material into separate reference files.
anti_patterns: Do not run copied hook scripts without reading each command first.
Do not leave placeholder names or generic descriptions in published extensions.
Do not add broad tool permissions unless the extension requires them.

faq.question: Does this skill create finished Claude Code extensions?
faq.answer: No. It provides starter templates that require manual editing, review, and testing.

faq.question: Which extension types are covered?
faq.answer: It includes templates for agents, skills, slash commands, and hook scripts.

faq.question: Can Codex use these templates?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex can use the template content to draft compatible files, but Claude Code behavior should be tested separately.

faq.question: Is the hook script safe to run immediately?
faq.answer: No. Review and customize the script before enabling it in a Claude Code project.

faq.question: Does the skill require network access?
faq.answer: No. The listed URLs are documentation references, not active network calls.

faq.question: Where do the templates get installed?
faq.answer: The documentation shows Claude Code paths for agents, commands, skills, and project hooks.
